headlines.title: New details on shooting suspect’s alleged planning
headlines.description: Prosecutors revealed new details of how a suspect allegedly planned to carry out his shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner. NBC News’ Kelly O’Donnell reports.

headlines.title: Trump recalls being evacuated after WHCD gunshots
headlines.description: Trump recalls being evacuated after WHCD gunshots

headlines.title: White House condemns 'political violence' after correspondents' dinner shooting
headlines.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt began a press briefing by thanking the law enforcement response to the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and condemning "political violence" and rising rhetoric in the country.

headlines.title: Suspect Cole Tomas Allen charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump
headlines.description: The man accused of opening fire outside the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner was charged with three counts in his first court appearance, including attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump. Cole Tomas Allen also faces charges of interstate transportation of weapons and discharge of a firearm during a violent crime.

headlines.title: Trump and first lady rip Jimmy Kimmel, call on ABC to 'take a stand'
headlines.description: First lady Melania Trump sharply criticized Jimmy Kimmel on Monday, blasting the ABC late-night host’s “hateful and violent rhetoric.”

headlines.title: Jane Fonda free speech group to ABC: Resist Trump, keep Kimmel
headlines.description: The Committee for the First Amendment, a free speech advocacy group led by actor Jane Fonda, criticized President Donald Trump for urging ABC to fire late-night host Jimmy Kimmel over his comments about first lady Melania Trump.“In America, satire is not a crime.

headlines.title: Correspondents' dinner conspiracy theories spread so fast that even Trump was alarmed
headlines.description: A flood of misinformation, conspiracy theories and false claims about the shooting at Saturday night’s White House Correspondents' Association dinner spread online at a notable and concerningly speedy rate, mostly about the baseless theory that the incident had been staged.

headlines.title: Group says it won't drop its White House ballroom lawsuit, despite DOJ pressure
headlines.description: The National Trust for Historic Preservation said it will not drop its lawsuit to halt construction of Trump's White House ballroom, despite the Justice Department's request.

headlines.title: What we know about the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
headlines.description: An armed man exchanged gunfire with law enforcement after he rushed a security checkpoint at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington, D.C., on Saturday, authorities said.

headlines.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive in Washington to meet with President Trump and First Lady
headlines.description: England’s king and queen are in the US as part of a reset with the president after recent tensions with the UK surrounding the war in Iran. The royal couple is here to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary. NBC News’ Hallie Jackson has the latest on their visit

headlines.title: King Charles, Queen Camilla Begin Historic State Visit to US
headlines.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla's high-profile state visit will proceed as planned following the shooting at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. The visit, celebrating 250 years of American independence and aimed at reinforcing the bond between the two allies, will mark Charles' first visit since taking the throne. NBC’s Erin McLaughlin reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Ron DeSantis releases new congressional map creating four more GOP-leaning seats in Florida
headlines.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. — Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis on Monday released a proposed redraw of the state’s congressional lines that, on paper, would create four new GOP-leaning seats — an aggressive proposal that the governor is using to try to challenge anti-gerrymandering language in the state Constitution
